name: Gematria engine ob3ect: the Hebrew letter-number correspondence where each letter has a numerical value, and words with equal values share a mystical connection. FSPLIT: a word is decomposed into its constituent letters, splitting unity into numerical values (e.g., Chai = Chet(8) + Yod(10) = 18). FFUSE: the numerical sum reconstitutes the word's essence, and words sharing the same sum (e.g., Chai=18 and Echad(One)=13... wait, finding a genuine pair requires same gematria). The genuine pair: Aleph(1)+Mem(40)+Tav(400)=441 = Emet (Truth), and the same sum 441 is also the square of 21 which is Eheyeh — Truth and Being share a numerical identity. ENGAGR: a word that has two different gematria values depending on whether final letters are counted — simultaneously one number and another.

phase_0.domain_name: Gematria engine ob3ect: the Hebrew letter-number correspondence where each letter has a numerical value, and words with equal values share a mystical connection. FSPLIT: a word is decomposed into its constituent letters, splitting unity into numerical values (e.g., Chai = Chet(8) + Yod(10) = 18). FFUSE: the numerical sum reconstitutes the word's essence, and words sharing the same sum (e.g., Chai=18 and Echad(One)=13... wait, finding a genuine pair requires same gematria). The genuine pair: Aleph(1)+Mem(40)+Tav(400)=441 = Emet (Truth), and the same sum 441 is also the square of 21 which is Eheyeh — Truth and Being share a numerical identity. ENGAGR: a word that has two different gematria values depending on whether final letters are counted — simultaneously one number and another.
phase_0.boundary_condition: The 22-letter Hebrew alphabet plus 5 final forms, the closed set of all possible letter combinations

VINIT.chosen_element: The unspoken, unlettered void before any letter is assigned a value
VINIT.justification: Void/uninitialized state before any letter or number exists

TANCH.chosen_element: The number 400 (Tav), the largest standard gematria value, the boundary of the system
TANCH.justification: Terminal anchor that contains the numerical range 1-400

AFWD.chosen_element: The act of reading a word left-to-right, accumulating letter values
AFWD.justification: Forward morphism from letters to their numerical sum

AREV.chosen_element: The act of decomposing a sum back into its constituent letters (e.g., 18 → Chet+Yod)
AREV.justification: Reverse/descent transformation from number to letters

CLINK.chosen_element: Concatenation of letters to form a word, e.g., Aleph+Mem+Tav = Emet
CLINK.justification: Composition of letters into a word via sequential chaining

IMSCRIB.chosen_element: A letter whose numerical value equals itself (e.g., Yod=10, the letter is its own number)
IMSCRIB.justification: Self-reference: the letter and its value are identical

FSPLIT.chosen_element: Decomposition of a word into its individual letters with their numerical values
FSPLIT.justification: Word splits into letters, e.g., Chai splits into Chet(8) and Yod(10)

FFUSE.chosen_element: Summation of the numerical values of the letters to reconstitute the word's total gematria
FFUSE.justification: 8+10=18 reconstitutes Chai's essence; the sum is the word's identity

EVALT.chosen_element: Two words sharing the same gematria sum (e.g., Chai=18 and Echad=13? No — genuine pair: Emet=441 and Eheyeh=21^2=441)
EVALT.justification: Affirmative: numerical identity reveals mystical connection

EVALF.chosen_element: Two words with different gematria sums that are mistakenly thought equal
EVALF.justification: Negative: false equivalence, no mystical link

ENGAGR.chosen_element: A word with two gematria values (standard vs. final-letter form), e.g., Mem=40 or 600
ENGAGR.justification: Paradox: simultaneously one number and another, both true

IFIX.chosen_element: The fixed gematria table (letter→number mapping) that never changes
IFIX.justification: Permanent, append-only record of the letter-number correspondence

phase_2.split_element: Decomposition of a word into its constituent letters with numerical values
phase_2.fuse_element: Summation of the numerical values of the letters

phase_3.void_description: 00 — No letter, no number, no word; the uncreated
phase_3.true_description: 01 — Two words share the same gematria sum, affirming mystical connection
phase_3.false_description: 10 — Two words have different sums, no connection
phase_3.both_description: 11 — A word with two valid gematria values (standard and final-letter), both true

steps.domain_action: Step 1 IMSCRIB: The first letter Aleph recognizes itself as 1
Step 2 AREV: From the number 1, descend to the letter Aleph
Step 3 FSPLIT: The word Emet splits into Aleph(1), Mem(40), Tav(400)
Step 4 AFWD: Sum the values 1+40+400 to ascend to 441
Step 5 FFUSE: 441 reconstitutes the word Emet
Step 6 CLINK: Compose Emet with Eheyeh (21^2=441) to form a mystical link
Step 7 IFIX: Record the letter-number table permanently
Step 8 IMSCRIB: The system recognizes itself as the complete gematria engine

phase_5.compiler_frontend: The tradition (Masorah) that defines letter values and final forms
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Numerical equivalence — words communicate by sharing the same sum
phase_5.memory_mechanism: The fixed gematria table stored in sacred texts
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: The order of letters in the alphabet (Aleph to Tav) determines sequence
phase_5.alfs_store: Sefer Yetzirah (Book of Formation) — the canonical bootstrap of Hebrew letters

phase_6.pre_cycle_state: A word in its unanalyzed form
phase_6.post_cycle_state: The word's gematria sum and its decomposed letters
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the sum of parts equals the whole; no information is lost
